$doc: Loop driver for Lede v2 (provider-agnostic/BYOK/auth/Drizzle spec).
Phase 0 detailed (it IS the whole risk, §24); Phases 1-4 coarse epics,
decomposed as Phase 0 teaches the shape. Ready/batches/breaches come from
schedule.mjs, never by eye. files is a CONTRACT (verifier fails undeclared
touches; package.json + bun.lock allowlisted). Since nothing type-checks
without T001's tsconfig, every ticket transitively depends on T001.
Acceptance hardened by the intake red-team (ledger [v2-004]) — contrast
checks, not existence checks. Two structural holes closed: (A) fixture
provenance via the shared eval-core T016 + the Phase-0 live-reproduces-
fixtures gate; (B) tag-scoring via the tag-shuffle invariance control.

tickets.title: Replace Bun scaffold with Fastify + Vite/React skeleton (bun toolchain)
tickets.context: Establish the §21 layout on the LOCKED stack (§3.1) but with the BUN toolchain (oracle.md 'Toolchain'): Fastify 4 API + Vite/React 18 SPA, one process. DELETE the existing Bun.serve scaffold (src/main.ts, src/routes/health.ts) — replaced, do not keep Bun.serve. Set up: tsconfig (client, ESM, react-jsx, path alias @shared/* → src/shared/*, include src/**), tsconfig.server.json, vite.config.ts (react plugin, @shared alias, dev proxy /api → http://localhost:8787), index.html mounting src/client/main.tsx (bare App shell), src/client/styles/{app.css,print.css} stubs. src/server/index.ts: minimal Fastify server booting on PORT (default 8787, §17) serving GET /api/health → {ok:true} (in prod also serves dist/ static — TODO stub fine). package.json scripts (bun): dev (concurrently dev:api+dev:web), dev:api (bun --watch src/server/index.ts), dev:web (vite), build (vite build), check (tsc --noEmit both configs), start (bun src/server/index.ts), test (vitest run). Deps per §20 (fastify, react, react-dom, vite, @vitejs/plugin-react, vitest, concurrently, typescript). Locked: PORT 8787, Fastify not Bun.serve, ESM, @shared.
tickets.acceptance:
- `bun install` succeeds; `bun run check` passes; `bun run build` emits dist/index.html
- server boots (`bun run start`); `curl localhost:8787/api/health` served by the running Fastify instance → `{"ok":true}`
- src/main.ts & src/routes/health.ts DO NOT exist; `grep -r 'Bun.serve' src` → no hits; src/server/index.ts imports fastify
- meta-check: tsconfig include covers src/**, so a deliberate type error in src FAILS `bun run check`
- an `@shared/*` import resolves under BOTH check and build; vite proxies /api → :8787
- pure scaffold: no behavior tests required (stated)
tickets.evidence: INDEPENDENT re-verify (coordinator, 2026-07-02): `bun run check`→exit 0; `bun run build`→dist/index.html + assets, exit 0; `bun run test`→'No test files found, exiting with code 0' (passWithNoTests, no dummy test); server boot `curl localhost:8787/api/health`→{"ok":true}. Scope: 14 touched paths all ⊆ declared ∪ {package.json,bun.lock}; no drift. Gaming read: test script = 'vitest run --passWithNoTests' (real green-on-empty), tsconfig include=['src/**/*'] (meta-check holds), src/server/index.ts imports fastify, no Bun.serve. Needed one re-dispatch to make the test runner green-on-empty.

tickets.title: Shared domain types (Entry, EntryMeta, TailorDecision, TailoredResume)
tickets.context: Author src/shared/types.ts EXACTLY per §4.1 and §5: Section (10-member union), EntryMeta (discriminated union on `section`), Entry (id, section, meta, facts=FACT-LOCK, tags, framings?, sortKey), Profile (§4.2 shape), ProviderId ('anthropic'|'openai'|'google'|'openai-compatible'), JDSignals, TailorDecision (the MODEL's flat output — signals, summary, items[{entryId,text,rank,leadRationale?}], cut[]), TailoredItem/Group/Section/Resume (SERVER-assembled), Layout = { section: Section|'summary'; enabled: boolean }[]. Imported by both server and client via @shared. Locked (oracle): model returns judgment only; facts are the fact-lock; tags never for scoring.
tickets.acceptance:
- `bun run check` passes
- types match §4.1/§5 field-for-field (EntryMeta discriminated on section; TailorDecision flat with per-item rank; TailoredResume is the assembled shape)
- types-only ticket: no runtime tests required (stated)
tickets.evidence: INDEPENDENT re-verify (coordinator, 2026-07-02): `bun run check`→exit 0; `bun run test`→exit 0 (green-on-empty, no test file added). Scope: only src/shared/types.ts touched. Correctness read: file matches spec §4.1/§5 field-for-field — EntryMeta is a discriminated union on `section` (10 variants), TailorDecision is FLAT (signals/summary/items[]{entryId,text,rank,leadRationale?}/cut[]), TailoredResume is the nested assembled shape (sections→groups→items); Profile/Layout/ProviderId/JDSignals all exported. No runtime code, no gaming.

tickets.title: Section registry (SECTIONS)
tickets.context: Author src/shared/sections.ts: the SECTIONS: Record<Section, {label; rephrase:'full'|'light'|'none'; order:'recency'|'relevance'|'manual'; groupBy?}> registry VERBATIM per §4.3 (experience/project full+recency+groupBy; education light+recency+groupBy; award/cert/publication recency; reference manual; skill none+relevance+groupBy category; interest relevance; language manual). Export SECTION_VALUES (the 10 keys). SINGLE SOURCE (§17) for rephrase/order/group — consumed by prompt, assemble, render — must be exact.
tickets.acceptance:
- `bun run check` passes; `bun run test` green (config-only, test-exempt per baseline — nothing behavioral to test; consumed by T008 assemble tests later)
- correctness (coordinator diff-read vs §4.3): SECTIONS has ALL 10 sections, each with the exact rephrase/order/groupBy triple, and SECTION_VALUES exports the 10 keys
- do NOT add a test file (not in declared files)
tickets.evidence: INDEPENDENT re-verify (fan-out workflow Verify+Gate, re-confirmed by coordinator on merged tree): check/build/test green. src/shared/sections.ts has all 10 sections with exact §4.3 rephrase/order/groupBy triples; SECTION_VALUES exports 10 keys. Diff-read confirms verbatim §4.3. Config-only, test-exempt.

tickets.title: Providers registry (PROVIDERS, resolveModel, providerOptionsFor)
tickets.context: Author src/shared/providers.ts per §6.1: PROVIDERS registry mapping each ProviderId → { label, models[], default }. resolveModel({provider,model,apiKey,baseURL}) builds an AI SDK LanguageModel via createAnthropic/createOpenAI/createGoogleGenerativeAI (openai-compatible = createOpenAI+baseURL). providerOptionsFor(provider) returns provider-specific extras (Anthropic: thinking/effort + prompt-cache control; sane defaults elsewhere). Deps: ai, @ai-sdk/anthropic, @ai-sdk/openai, @ai-sdk/google. Default Anthropic claude-opus-4-8; include a Google model. PHASE-0 EVAL MODEL = gemini-2.5-flash (ledger [v2-005]: key tested live — flash works incl. structured output; gemini-2.5-pro is 429 quota-blocked/no-billing). The Google factory ALSO reads GOOGLE_GENERATIVE_AI_API_KEY from env — Phase 0 relies on that.
tickets.acceptance:
- `bun run check` + `bun run test` pass
- parametrized over ALL 4 ProviderIds: each returned model's provider/modelId matches the REQUESTED provider (dispatch correctness — not just non-throw)
- the passed apiKey is forwarded to the factory (not dropped); google resolves via GOOGLE_GENERATIVE_AI_API_KEY env alone
- providerOptionsFor('anthropic') includes cache-control/thinking keys; other providers return defined sane defaults
tickets.evidence: INDEPENDENT re-verify (workflow Verify+coordinator): check/build green; test/providers.test.ts 14/14. resolveModel dispatch correct for all 4 providers (modelId exact; provider strings distinct incl. openai vs openai-compatible); apiKey forwarded (verified via AI SDK header closure, no network); google resolves via GOOGLE_GENERATIVE_AI_API_KEY env; providerOptionsFor('anthropic') has cacheControl+thinking. Deps ai@7/@ai-sdk/* added. No tag-scoring.

tickets.title: zod schemas: TailorDecisionZ + entry/meta validation
tickets.context: Author src/shared/schema.ts. Critical: TailorDecisionZ — hand-written zod for the model's flat TailorDecision (§5), passed straight to generateObject. Also entryMetaZ (STRICT discriminatedUnion on 'section', one object per §4.1 — reject foreign fields) and entryInput (plain zod for Phase 0: id?, section enum, meta=entryMetaZ, facts 1-300 x≤12, tags ≤8 x1-40, framings? ≤6 x1-200, sortKey int; superRefine meta.section===section + per-section facts arity per §17). drizzle-zod derivation deferred to Phase 1 (no Drizzle yet). Locked: TailorDecisionZ hand-written, never table-derived.
tickets.acceptance:
- `bun run check` + `bun run test` pass
- Rejection table (each a distinct assert): TailorDecisionZ REJECTS empty entryId, non-numeric rank, missing signals.hardRequirements, a cut entry missing reason; ACCEPTS one fully-populated valid decision
- Strict meta: canonical experience meta PASSES; same object + foreign field (school) REJECTED; missing role REJECTED
- Facts arity: skill with 2 facts REJECTED (label sections exactly 1); experience with 0 facts REJECTED (narrative ≥1); certification with [] facts PASSES; meta.section !== section REJECTED
tickets.evidence: INDEPENDENT re-verify (workflow Verify+Gate, re-confirmed on merged tree): check/build green; test/schema.test.ts 13/13. TailorDecisionZ rejects empty entryId / non-numeric rank / missing signals.hardRequirements / cut missing reason, accepts a valid decision; entryMetaZ strict (foreign 'school' field rejected, missing role rejected); facts arity (skill!=2, experience>=1, cert [] ok, meta.section mismatch rejected). zod dep added. Hand-written, not table-derived.

tickets.title: Phase 0 seed entries (SEED_ENTRIES)
tickets.context: Author src/server/seed.ts exporting SEED_ENTRIES: Entry[] VERBATIM from §22 — the three Cloudcase experience entries (cloudcase-rules-engine sortKey 202101, cloudcase-frontend-rewrite 202501, cloudcase-platform-sdk 202503). All tagged platform-arch ON PURPOSE (forces discrimination on FACTS not tags) and all ONE job (same company/role/period → one group, so every flip is the group's leading item). Copy facts/tags/framings/sortKey EXACTLY — do NOT edit facts to inject target keywords (that would make the flip trivial keyword-lookup and void the Tier-0 premise). NOTE the secondary-tag hole (sdk/frontend/delivery map 1:1 to targets) is neutralized downstream by the tag-shuffle control (T014/T015), not by the seed.
tickets.acceptance:
- `bun run check` passes; `bun run test` green (data-only, test-exempt per baseline — verified by diff-read; consumed by T013/T014 later)
- correctness (coordinator diff-read vs §22): SEED_ENTRIES deep-equals the §22 literal — the 3 ids (cloudcase-rules-engine/-frontend-rewrite/-platform-sdk), meta, facts element-for-element, tags, framings, sortKey
- all three share company/role/period (one group); every entry's tags include platform-arch; sortKeys exactly 202101/202501/202503 (the OLDEST, rules-engine, can still lead — proving rank not recency); facts NOT edited to inject target keywords
- do NOT add a test file (not in declared files)
tickets.evidence: INDEPENDENT re-verify (workflow Verify+coordinator diff-read): check/build/test green. src/server/seed.ts SEED_ENTRIES = §22 literal element-for-element (3 ids, sortKeys 202101/202501/202503, all one Cloudcase group, all tags incl platform-arch, facts NOT keyword-injected, framings only on rules-engine). Data-only, test-exempt.

tickets.title: System prompt + renderLibrary (the product IP)
tickets.context: Author src/server/tailor/prompt.ts — THE product IP (§6.2). SYSTEM_PROMPT (frozen; never interpolate JD/timestamp) encodes: judge from FACTS not tags; the fact-lock (never invent, never strengthen — 'contributed to' ≠ 'led'); the rephrase policy full/light/none GENERATED from SECTIONS (so prompt/code never drift); rank-within-group = the lede; a leadRationale on each group's leading item tied to a NAMED JD signal; return ONLY the flat TailorDecision with every number grounded in facts. renderLibrary(entries) serializes deterministically (by section, then sortKey desc, then id; stable key order) and FOREGROUNDS facts (tags not presented as a scorable field). This prompt is what makes the §22 lede flip — if the eval (T014) doesn't flip sharply, THIS file is the bug, not the code.
tickets.acceptance:
- `bun run check` + `bun run test` pass
- Derivation not literal: the policy block is produced by a pure fn(SECTIONS); a test passing a SECTIONS copy with one section's rephrase FLIPPED asserts the rendered block reflects the flipped value (a hardcoded string fails this)
- Order-independence: SCRAMBLED input entries → output equals the canonical section→sortKey-desc→id ordering, and two different input orderings yield BYTE-IDENTICAL output
- Facts foregrounded: renderLibrary(e) output contains every string in e.facts; tags are not framed as 'match these'
- SYSTEM_PROMPT contains no JD/timestamp interpolation
tickets.evidence: INDEPENDENT re-verify (workflow Verify+coordinator): check/build green; test/prompt.test.ts 9/9. SYSTEM_PROMPT frozen (no JD/timestamp interpolation), encodes facts-not-tags + fact-lock ('contributed to'≠'led') + rank=lede + leadRationale-names-signal. Rephrase policy DERIVED from SECTIONS via pure fn (flip test: changing a section's rephrase in a SECTIONS copy changes the rendered block — not a hardcoded literal). renderLibrary deterministic (section→sortKey desc→id, byte-identical across input orderings), facts foregrounded, tags labeled 'never a relevance signal'. NOTE: real proof of the flip is the live eval T014.

tickets.title: assemble() — deterministic structure from the flat decision
tickets.context: Author src/server/tailor/assemble.ts: assemble(decision, entries, layout, sections): TailoredResume. Server owns ALL structure (§5): group by registry groupBy; group/flat order by registry `order` (recency = MAX member sortKey desc, server-computed; relevance = ascending min member rank; manual = sortKey); items-WITHIN-group ALWAYS by rank (lowest = lede); coerce text for rephrase:'none' to facts verbatim (empty-facts → text:''); filter to sections enabled in layout. The model NEVER controls structure. Keyless-testable — the regression net's core.
tickets.acceptance:
- `bun run check` + `bun run test` pass
- Max-member contrast: group A sortKeys [202101,202503] supplied in that order, group B [202501] → A ordered before B (A's key = max = 202503, distinguishing max from min/first)
- Coercion is an OVERRIDE not pass-through: a rephrase:'none' entry fed decision text:"FABRICATED" → output text is the verbatim fact, not "FABRICATED"; empty-facts entry → text:""
- Section order follows LAYOUT: layout orders B before A while entries inserted A before B → output sections [B,A]; an enabled:false section present in entries is absent
- items within a group order by rank (lowest first); flipping rank flips the leading item
tickets.evidence: INDEPENDENT re-verify (workflow Verify+coordinator): check/build green; test/assemble.test.ts 7/7. Server owns structure: recency groups by MAX member sortKey desc (contrast test distinguishes max from min/first); rephrase:'none' text COERCED to facts verbatim (decision text 'FABRICATED' overridden; empty-facts→''); section order follows LAYOUT (permuted) + disabled sections dropped; items within group by rank (flip test). Deterministic core of the keyless regression net.

tickets.title: validateNoFabrication + extractNumbers/hasNumberToken
tickets.context: Author src/server/tailor/validate.ts per §6.3: validateNoFabrication(resume, entries, baseSummary?) throws FabricationError on (a) an item entryId not a real entry, (b) a number in item.text not present in THAT item's OWN entry facts (per-entry scoped, not pooled), (c) a summary number not traceable to a kept entry's facts OR baseSummary. extractNumbers/hasNumberToken compare WHOLE number tokens INCLUDING attached %/k/x/units (so 30k≠30, 50%≠50), separators normalized (1,000==1000), NOT substrings ('1' ≠ inside '2021'). Mechanical only (§23) — NO LLM-checks-LLM pass, ever.
tickets.acceptance:
- `bun run check` + `bun run test` pass
- Token semantics (one assert per pair): text "30k" PASSES only if facts contain "30k", THROWS if facts contain only "30"; "50%" does NOT match a fact of "50"; "1,000" matches a fact of "1000"
- Per-entry scoping: an item whose number is present in a DIFFERENT entry's facts but absent from its OWN entry THROWS
- Detection completeness: a fake number injected into a DEEP location (summary, or a buried non-leading item in a late group) still THROWS and names the invented token (proves all items scanned)
- clean output PASSES; unknown entryId THROWS; summary number grounded in baseSummary PASSES
tickets.evidence: INDEPENDENT re-verify (workflow Verify+coordinator): check/build green; test/validate.test.ts 11/11. Whole-token semantics ('30k'≠'30','50%'≠'50','1,000'=='1000','1' not in '2021'); per-entry scoping enforced; deep-buried fake number throws naming the token; unknown entryId throws; baseSummary-grounded summary passes. Mechanical only, no LLM call.

tickets.title: Shared eval-core: hash(jd,entries), flip-predicate, canonical JDs, tag-shuffle
tickets.origin: red-team Finding A (fixture provenance) + Finding B (tag-scoring); spec §22
tickets.context: SINGLE SOURCE that closes the two structural holes. Author src/server/tailor/evalcore.ts exporting: (1) hashKey(jd, entries) — the ONE fixture key used by BOTH FixtureEngine (T010) and record-fixtures (T013), so a fixture can't be keyed one way and looked up another; (2) CONTRAST_JDS — the 3 §22 JDs, authored so NONE contains any entry id or a fact substring ≥ ~12 chars from its target (no keyword leakage), each mapped to its expected target (platform-sdk/rules-engine/frontend-rewrite); (3) flipPredicate(resume: TailoredResume, targetId) → { leads, rationaleNamesSignal } — target is its group's UNIQUE lowest-rank leading item AND its group.leadRationale contains a token from resume.signals; used identically by eval, record-fixtures, and the keyless suite; (4) tagShuffle(entries) — returns entries with tags permuted/stripped across entries (for the invariance control). This module is imported by T010, T013, T014, T015 — the provenance chain's spine.
tickets.acceptance:
- `bun run check` + `bun run test` pass
- hashKey is deterministic and order-insensitive over entries; hashKey(CONTRAST_JDS[i], SEED_ENTRIES) is stable
- unit test: none of CONTRAST_JDS contains an entry id or a ≥12-char fact substring from its target (guards keyword leakage)
- flipPredicate returns leads=true only when target is the UNIQUE minimum-rank leading item of its group; false on a rank tie or when target is in cut
- tagShuffle preserves entry ids/facts/sortKeys and only permutes tags
tickets.evidence: INDEPENDENT re-verify (coordinator): check/build green; test/evalcore.test.ts adds 14 (68 total). hashKey deterministic + order-insensitive (sha256 over jd+entries-sorted-by-id); ANTI-LEAKAGE verified — each CONTRAST_JDS jd contains neither its target id nor any ≥12-char substring of that target's facts (builder's drafts leaked 'integration path'/'the codebase'/'consistent patterns' and were reworded until clean). JDs read as realistic employer descriptions of the NEED, not keyword lists. flipPredicate: leads = target is its group's items[0] (leading) & not in cut; rationaleNamesSignal = leadRationale references a resume.signals token (true/false/filler/empty cases tested). tagShuffle permutes tags across entries, preserves id/facts/sortKey, no mutation. No new deps. Scope: only evalcore.ts + test.

tickets.title: TailorEngine (Fixture + Provider) + tailor() orchestration
tickets.context: Author src/server/tailor/engine.ts per §6.1: interface TailorEngine { decide(jd, entries): Promise<TailorDecision> }. ProviderEngine (real): resolveModel(cfg) then generateObject({ model, schema: TailorDecisionZ, system: SYSTEM_PROMPT + renderLibrary(entries), prompt: 'Tailor for this job description:\n\n'+jd, providerOptions: providerOptionsFor(provider) }); retry EXACTLY once on failure then throw. FixtureEngine (keyless): loadFixtureDecision by hashKey(jd, entries) FROM T016 (shared) out of test/fixtures/decisions; a MISS throws a no_fixture-tagged error (→422), NEVER a silent fallback / first-file. tailor(engine, jd, entries, layout, baseSummary?): decision = engine.decide → assemble → validateNoFabrication → return (engine-agnostic). Engine factory by env LEDE_TAILOR_ENGINE (live|fixture; fixture default under NODE_ENV=test). The engine split is what makes everything except the live call keyless.
tickets.acceptance:
- `bun run check` + `bun run test` pass
- Validate-wired contrast: tailor() with a stub engine returning a decision containing a FABRICATED number THROWS FabricationError; same stub with a clean decision returns a resume (proves validate runs, not just assemble)
- Hash keying with ≥2 fixtures: decide(jdA)→fixtureA, decide(jdB)→fixtureB (distinct); decide(jdC unrecorded) THROWS no_fixture WHILE fixtures exist (closes first-file/silent-fallback)
- Retry count: a mock model throwing once then succeeding → decide resolves after EXACTLY 2 calls; throwing twice → rejects after 2
- Engine factory returns FixtureEngine under NODE_ENV=test and ProviderEngine under live (assert by type); ProviderEngine calls generateObject with schema===TailorDecisionZ and a system string containing renderLibrary(entries) output
tickets.evidence: INDEPENDENT re-verify (coordinator): check/build green; test/engine.test.ts 10 (78 total). tailor() = decide→assemble→validateNoFabrication (validate WIRED, contrast test: fabricated '9999%' throws FabricationError, clean returns resume). ProviderEngine retry EXACTLY once (try/catch/retry, generateObject ≤2 calls, asserted). FixtureEngine keys by evalcore.hashKey; MISS throws NoFixtureError code 'no_fixture' listing scenarios (no silent fallback / first-file). makeEngine: fixture under NODE_ENV=test/LEDE_TAILOR_ENGINE=fixture, ProviderEngine (google/gemini-2.5-flash) under live. Wiring test asserts generateObject called with schema===TailorDecisionZ + system containing renderLibrary output. Scope: only engine.ts+test.

tickets.title: Fastify /api/tailor + /api/health + config (Phase 0, no DB/auth)
tickets.context: Extend src/server/index.ts (from T001) + add src/server/config.ts. config.ts: fail-fast env loader (§17) — PORT (8787), LEDE_TAILOR_ENGINE (live|fixture). Phase 0 does NOT require LEDE_MASTER_KEY/SESSION_SECRET yet (arrive with auth/BYOK in Phase 2). Route POST /api/tailor: zod-validate { jobDescription } (1-20000, §17); build engine per config (live=ProviderEngine using the Google bootstrap key from env; fixture=FixtureEngine); call tailor(engine, jd, SEED_ENTRIES, defaultLayout) — the route MUST go through tailor() (assemble+validate), never return a raw decision or canned resume. Error mapping (§9): provider auth→'key invalid', rate-limit→429, other provider fail→502, zod-fail on LLM output→502, fixture miss→422 no_fixture. GET /api/health → {ok:true}.

tickets.title: Client Phase 0: JD paste → tailor → deterministic resume render
tickets.context: Minimal Phase 0 client (no shadcn/Tailwind yet — lands in E1; plain CSS here). api.ts: typed fetch to /api/tailor throwing ApiError{status,code,message}. TailorView: JDInput (textarea) + tailor button → /api/tailor → ResumePage. ResumePage: SINGLE-COLUMN deterministic render of TailoredResume (§10) — ProfileHeader stub, summary, then each TailoredSection (registry label + groups) in LAYOUT order (data-driven, not hardcoded). print.css (@media print): US-Letter, #000 on #fff, real text, no images/tables/multi-column. leadRationale/cut[] MUST NOT render on the resume page (§10) — reasoning UI is Phase 3. Wire into App.tsx.
tickets.acceptance:
- `bun run check` + `bun run build` pass
- Leak check: feed a TailoredResume fixture whose leadRationale and every cut[].reason contain UNIQUE SENTINEL strings; assert those exact strings are ABSENT from the rendered resume DOM textContent
- Data-driven order: use a NON-DEFAULT (permuted) layout; assert rendered section order equals layout order (not hardcoded); items within a group follow given order
- print.css @media print: no column-count/columns>1, no display:table/table layout, #000 on #fff (grep-checkable)

tickets.title: record-fixtures script + recorded decision fixtures (KEY-GATED)
tickets.context: Author scripts/record-fixtures.ts: runs the LIVE ProviderEngine (Google bootstrap key, GOOGLE_GENERATIVE_AI_API_KEY) over T016's CONTRAST_JDS against SEED_ENTRIES and writes each TailorDecision to test/fixtures/decisions/<name>.json keyed by T016 hashKey(jd, SEED_ENTRIES). Fails LOUD (non-zero, no key). BEFORE writing each fixture it runs the decision through tailor()→assemble→validateNoFabrication AND T016 flipPredicate — a non-flipping or fabricating decision CANNOT be written. Writes manifest.json (model id, prompt hash, timestamp, token usage) — provenance. These committed fixtures are what makes the keyless suite (T015) replay the REAL scenarios; a hand-authored fixture would not survive the live-reproduces-fixtures phase gate (oracle Phase 0).
tickets.acceptance:
- `bun run scripts/record-fixtures.ts` (with GOOGLE key) writes 3 fixtures + manifest.json; each recorded decision is TailorDecisionZ-valid, passes validateNoFabrication, and passes T016 flipPredicate for its target
- fixture key = T016 hashKey(jd, SEED_ENTRIES); a subsequent FixtureEngine.decide(jd, SEED_ENTRIES) returns exactly that fixture (shared-hash proof)
- across the 3 fixtures the rank-1 (leading) entry does NOT track sortKey order (proves a real rank flip, not recency)
- each fixture embeds/matches the canonical JD text and the SEED_ENTRIES hash (guards library/JD swap)

tickets.title: Phase 0 acceptance oracle — scripts/eval.ts (KEY-GATED, THE gate)
tickets.context: Author scripts/eval.ts — THE Phase 0 behavioral oracle (§22, §25). Constructs ProviderEngine EXPLICITLY (Google bootstrap key, model gemini-2.5-flash per ledger [v2-005] — pro is quota-blocked) and EXITS NON-ZERO if no key (never skip-as-pass, never fall back to FixtureEngine). Runs the live model over T016 CONTRAST_JDS against EXACTLY SEED_ENTRIES via tailor(), using T016 flipPredicate. Exit 0 iff all 3 flip correctly, non-zero (naming the miss) otherwise. Locked: if it doesn't flip sharply, the PROMPT (§6.2, T007) is wrong — not the code.
tickets.acceptance:
- `bun run scripts/eval.ts` (with GOOGLE_GENERATIVE_AI_API_KEY) exits 0; asserts engine instanceof ProviderEngine and ≥1 live response with token usage
- over exactly SEED_ENTRIES (length===3, ids match): for each JD the expected target is present in items (NOT cut) and is the STRICT UNIQUE minimum rank
- the three leading entryIds are MUTUALLY DISTINCT and equal the SET {cloudcase-platform-sdk, cloudcase-rules-engine, cloudcase-frontend-rewrite} (set assertion, not 3 isolated checks)
- each target's leadRationale contains a token from that JD's decision.signals; the three rationales are pairwise different
- TAG-SHUFFLE CONTROL (Finding B): re-run over tagShuffle(SEED_ENTRIES); the flip is UNCHANGED (if shuffling tags changes the lede → tag-scoring → FAIL)
- exits non-zero naming the failing JD when a lede does not flip

tickets.title: Keyless pipeline e2e tests (FixtureEngine replay)
tickets.origin: spec §18, §25 keyless guarantee; red-team Findings A+B
tickets.context: Author test/tailor.e2e.test.ts: drive tailor() AND POST /api/tailor with FixtureEngine over the recorded fixtures (T013) for the 3 CONTRAST_JDS — NO API KEY. Asserts the assembled resume's structure/order/coercion/validation AND that the correct target leads each JD (replaying the flip keylessly via T016 flipPredicate). The standing regression net (§25 keyless guarantee). Honest limit (record in ledger): this replays FROZEN decisions, so it proves the MACHINERY, not that the live model still flips — that rests on T014 being genuinely run. It also cannot catch a fabricated fixture; the anti-recency check below is the one recency-vs-rank cheat it CAN kill alone.
tickets.acceptance:
- `bun run test` (GOOGLE_GENERATIVE_AI_API_KEY UNSET) passes; assert the engine used is FixtureEngine
- for each of the 3 JDs the SPECIFIC expected target is its group's UNIQUE leading item, and the three leads are MUTUALLY DISTINCT
- Anti-recency: rules-engine JD → cloudcase-rules-engine leads (it has the OLDEST sortKey 202101, impossible under recency), and ≥2 of 3 leads are NOT the max-sortKey entry
- a specifically-UNRECORDED jd yields the tagged no_fixture error via tailor() and 422 {error:'no_fixture'} via HTTP
- structure-ignores-tags: replay with tagShuffle(SEED_ENTRIES) → identical assembled order/selection (proves assemble/render never let tags influence structure)

tickets.title: Phase 1 — Drizzle + SQLite persistence; entry library editor; profile; layout; shadcn/Tailwind foundation
tickets.context: COARSE EPIC — decompose when Phase 0 closes into: (a) shadcn/ui + Tailwind + §12 token theming foundation; (b) Drizzle schema src/server/db/schema.ts (§4.2: entries/profile/settings/secrets, JSON columns, singletons id=1+CHECK) + db/index.ts (better-sqlite3, WAL, DATA_DIR) + migrations run on boot + singleton INSERT OR IGNORE seeding (settings.layout defaults §4.2); (c) REPLACE Phase 0 plain-zod entry schema with drizzle-zod createInsertSchema (§4.4) — keep TailorDecisionZ hand-written and the §17 bounds (facts≤12,tags≤8,…) that derivation can't infer; (d) /api/entries CRUD + import/export, /api/profile, /api/settings (layout) — zod-validated (§9); (e) /api/tailor reads entries from DB; (f) client: TanStack Query + react-router, LibraryView + LibraryToolbar + SectionAccordion + EntryCard + EntryEditor (section-aware via registry, one editor for all sections) + SectionMetaFields + RepeatableList + TagInput + ProfileEditor + LayoutEditor. Sections first: experience/project/education/skill (§2). RED-TEAM — child tickets MUST include: (1) export = ENTRIES ONLY (assert payload has no ciphertext/plaintext key/auth hash/non-entry table); (2) real restart: process A writes → exit → process B reads same DATA_DIR (real .sqlite, not :memory:); (3) migrations from empty DATA_DIR create all 4 tables AND the CHECK constraints (query sqlite_master; INSERT id=2 into a singleton FAILS); (4) layout contrast: a NON-DEFAULT layout (reordered + one disabled) changes render order and drops the disabled section.
tickets.acceptance: See oracle.md Phase 1 (merged-tree): migrations on boot; entries CRUD persists across restart; import/export round-trip (secrets excluded); LibraryView create/edit/delete end-to-end; profile round-trip; layout ordering respected.

tickets.title: Phase 2 — auth gate + BYOK (encrypted key storage, validate-on-save, Settings)
tickets.context: COARSE EPIC — decompose after E1 into: (a) src/server/crypto.ts (AES-256-GCM encrypt/decrypt using LEDE_MASTER_KEY; scrypt hash/verify) + config.ts fail-fast REQUIRING LEDE_MASTER_KEY + LEDE_SESSION_SECRET at boot; (b) secrets-table access; (c) auth.ts + /api/auth/{setup,login,logout} (@fastify/secure-session) + session guard on ALL /api/* except /api/auth/* and /api/health; first-run set-password; LEDE_AUTH_DISABLED escape hatch (default false); (d) BYOK: PUT /api/settings/key (validate via one cheap live generateText BEFORE storing, then store ciphertext), DELETE, GET /api/settings returns {keySet,provider,model,baseUrl,layout} NEVER the key; (e) /api/tailor switches to ProviderEngine with the DECRYPTED BYOK key (in-memory only), no_api_key→400; (f) client: LoginGate, SettingsView + ApiKeyForm (write-only) + ProviderPicker + ModelPicker. RED-TEAM — child tickets MUST include: (1) SENTINEL scan — store key sk-SENTINEL-<uuid>, hit EVERY endpoint incl. tailor error paths, grep every response body + all stdout/stderr/log files + the .sqlite/-wal/-shm + entire DATA_DIR; sentinel appears nowhere plaintext, only in secrets.apiKeyEnc ciphertext; (2) master key load-bearing — decryption THROWS/garbles when LEDE_MASTER_KEY changed; two encryptions of same plaintext have DIFFERENT iv (catches base64/ECB fakes); (3) PROCESS-LEVEL boot refusal — spawn real server with LEDE_MASTER_KEY unset AND with a malformed key → non-zero exit, no listening port, does NOT auto-generate a master key; (4) master-key material never in DATA_DIR (the backup unit, §23); (5) validate-BEFORE-store — bad key → 4xx AND secrets.apiKeyEnc unchanged; (6) guard total with auth ENABLED — every route except auth/health returns 401 sans session (esp. /api/tailor).
tickets.acceptance: See oracle.md Phase 2 (merged-tree): boot refuses without LEDE_MASTER_KEY (process-level); encrypt/decrypt round-trip w/ distinct IVs; login→session, guarded routes 401; key stored as ciphertext, sentinel scan clean across responses/logs/DATA_DIR; validate-on-save; /api/tailor no-key→400 no_api_key.

tickets.title: Phase 3 — reasoning UI (timebox) + remaining sections
tickets.context: COARSE EPIC — decompose after E2 into: (a) ReasoningPanel split view (ResultView = ResumePage | ReasoningPanel): SignalsBar→WeightBar, per-group SectionRationale→serif Callout (leadRationale on each job's leading bullet), CutList from cut[]; (b) GUARANTEE leadRationale + cut[] NEVER in the printed resume DOM (§10); (c) remaining sections award/certification/publication/interest/language/reference as data + rendering. RED-TEAM — child tickets MUST include: (1) DOM-ABSENCE not display:none — feed a fixture with NON-EMPTY leadRationale + cut reasons, assert those exact strings are absent from the ACTUAL print-target subtree's textContent (a display:none-only impl FAILS; render the real print root, not ResumePage in isolation); (2) TIMEBOX ceiling — text callouts only, GREP-FORBID EventSource/SSE (non-MVP §15), animation libs (framer-motion), any new tailors/history table; cap reasoning-UI ticket count; (3) remaining sections — feed one entry of EACH new section and assert each renders with its registry label/order, and empty-facts entries (cert/reference) render from meta with text:''.
tickets.acceptance: See oracle.md Phase 3 (merged-tree): ReasoningPanel renders signals + per-group leadRationale + cut; leadRationale/cut ABSENT from the printed DOM tree (not merely hidden); remaining sections render from data.

tickets.title: Phase 4 — render polish + Docker/compose/README (self-host)
tickets.context: COARSE EPIC — decompose after E3 into: (a) print.css polish to ATS-safe (§10: single-column, #000/#fff, no tables/images/multi-column/borders, system serif/sans NOT Plex); (b) §12 design finish — tokens.css, shadcn theme mapped to tokens, self-hosted IBM Plex via @fontsource, low radius, --accent primary; (c) Dockerfile multi-stage (build SPA+server → slim BUN runtime image; deviation from §19 node-slim per toolchain decision) + docker-compose.yml (one service, volume at DATA_DIR) + .env.example with the two required secrets; migrations on boot; (d) README self-host first-run. RED-TEAM — child tickets MUST include: (1) DEEP boot check — after compose up, migrations ran (tables in the mounted volume) and GET / returns the built index.html (not a stub); (2) carry boot-refusal into the image — compose up with the two secrets ABSENT → container exits non-zero; NO real secret baked in image/compose (grep layers+compose; .env.example placeholders only); (3) VOLUME persistence — write data, compose down && up, data survives; (4) fonts self-hosted — grep build output for fonts.googleapis/CDN font URLs → ABSENT; @fontsource present; (5) print.css grep-checkable (no / in ResumePage, no multi-column in print target, #000/#fff) but TRUE ATS text-extraction/reading-order needs headless print — flag as only partially machine-checkable, requires manual/browser verification (state honestly).
tickets.acceptance: See oracle.md Phase 4 (merged-tree): print.css ATS-safe (grep + honest manual caveat); §12 tokens+shadcn+self-hosted Plex; `docker compose up` deep-boots with the two secrets (migrations ran, real SPA served), refuses without them, volume persists; README first-run documented.
